runs.summary: Master Services Agreement - Vendor Draft: 3 risk(s), 1 gap(s) and 2 mitigation action(s) identified. Top risk: Delayed service due to provider's discretion in notifying security incidents Main evidence gap: Owner for action items not specified
warnings: Owner for action items not specified
Open-ended questions may require additional human reading when the document has contextual ambiguity.

decision_summary.label: Renegotiate
decision_summary.summary: Multiple risks identified including delayed incident notification, limited audit rights, unclear liability cap, and missing action item ownership; requires legal review and renegotiation of key terms before approval.
decision_summary.due_date: within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred

watchouts: Owner for action items not specified
Open-ended questions may require additional human reading when the document has contextual ambiguity.
next_steps: Ensure data deletion policies are clear and enforceable
Negotiate tighter incident notification timelines and objective criteria for material incident determination.
Require explicit audit rights and define clear, enforceable data deletion timelines and verification mechanisms.

top_blockers.title: Missing ownership for risk mitigation actions
Delayed incident notification without objective triggers
Uncapped liability for data security claims

business_impact.label: Impact 1
business_impact.detail: Increased exposure to data breaches due to limited oversight and weak deletion terms

business_impact.label: Impact 2
business_impact.detail: Potential financial losses from inadequate liability caps

business_impact.label: Impact 3
business_impact.detail: Operational delays in incident response affecting service reliability

business_impact.label: Impact 4
business_impact.detail: Compliance risk from inability to verify vendor controls

findings.title: Delayed security incident notification
findings.description: Provider has discretion in determining when a material incident occurs and only notifies customer within 10 business days after that determination, which may delay response to critical issues.
findings.recommendation: Negotiate tighter incident notification timelines and objective criteria for material incident determination.
findings.snippet: Provider will notify Customer within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred.

findings.title: Lack of customer audit rights and weak data deletion obligations
findings.description: Customer lacks audit rights; provider may share only a summary letter or certification at its discretion, and data deletion post-termination relies on commercially reasonable efforts without a deadline.
findings.recommendation: Require explicit audit rights and define clear, enforceable data deletion timelines and verification mechanisms.
findings.snippet: No customer audit right. Provider may instead share a summary letter or certification at its discretion. Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination.

findings.title: Unclear liability cap for data security claims
findings.description: Aggregate liability is limited to fees paid in the prior 12 months, which may be insufficient to cover damages from data security or confidentiality breaches.
findings.recommendation: Negotiate a higher liability cap or carve-out for data security claims.
findings.snippet: Aggregate liability limited to fees paid in prior 12 months, including for confidentiality and data-security claims.

findings.title: Owner for action items not specified
findings.description: Recommended actions such as reviewing subprocessor list and ensuring data deletion policies lack assigned owners, creating accountability gaps.
findings.recommendation: Assign clear owners and due dates for all action items.
findings.snippet: Owner for action items not specified

evidence_trail.title: Delayed security incident notification
evidence_trail.snippet: Provider will notify Customer within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred.

evidence_trail.title: Lack of customer audit rights and weak data deletion obligations
evidence_trail.snippet: No customer audit right. Provider may instead share a summary letter or certification at its discretion. Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination.

evidence_trail.title: Unclear liability cap for data security claims
evidence_trail.snippet: Aggregate liability limited to fees paid in prior 12 months, including for confidentiality and data-security claims.

evidence_trail.title: Owner for action items not specified
evidence_trail.snippet: Owner for action items not specified
